hypothesis: Contrastive multimodal pretraining between SMILES and electron density grids will induce emergent enantiomer discrimination—distinguishing R from S stereoisomers—when the symbolic anchor is replaced with stereochemically explicit SMILES (e.g., using @/@ chirality notation), because the contrastive loss will force the 3D encoder to encode handedness-sensitive features of the electron density that differentiate mirror-image configurations sharing identical constitutional connectivity, a capability that canonical SMILES anchoring systematically suppresses by treating enantiomers as identical symbolic strings.
